titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 37

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=120 status=live modified=2017-01-30 19:28:00.814145+00 | translation moa=121 status=live modified=2016-06-06 15:40:12.68473+00

div.p:
Anno ab incarnatione domini . Millesimo . CC [Ducentesimo]. Quadragesimo septimo cum esset lis mota
inter dominum Johannem priorem ⁊ conuentum de sancto andree ex una parte super quadam terra quam
Robertus dictus King legauit in ultima uoluntate sua dictis canonicis deo ⁊ sancto
andre seruientibus pro anima sua ⁊ antecessorum ⁊ successorum suorum . Et Godam filiam fratris
nominati Roberti ex altera  talis conuentio facta est inter partes in plena curia . videlicet quod dicti
prior ⁊ conuentus dederunt dicte . Gode . pro exscambio triginta solidorum sterlingorum . ⁊ dederunt ⁊ etiam
concesserunt eidem . G . ⁊ heredibus suis quandam terram in uilla de aberden . quam Robertus pe
tith donauit nominatis canonicis pro deo ⁊ anima sua in sua propria potestate . uel redditum
prefate terre annuatim percipiendum . Reddendo annuatim de predicta terra nomine fraternitatis prefatis canonicis deo ⁊ sancto andree seruientibus vnam libram Cymini in nundinis apostolorum Pe
tri ⁊ Pauli . Dicta uero Goda iurauit in plena curia pro se ⁊ heredibus suis quod predicta om
nia fideliter obseruaret . ⁊ quod nominatam terram prefatis priori ⁊ canonicis contra omnes homines
⁊ feminas warantizabit . Prior autem ⁊ conuentus prenominati warantizabunt dicte . G . ⁊ here
dum . [–Et] ut predicta omnia fideliter ⁊ sine scrupulo doli obseruentur . Prior ⁊ conuentus commune
Sigillum Capituli parti compositionis que remanet apud Godam ⁊ heredes suos apponi fecerunt .
Et alteri parti appositum est sigillum Commune de uilla sancti andree . Testibus . Capitulo sancti andree .
⁊ curia eiusdem uille .

p.cl: In the One-thousand-two-hundred-and-forty-seventh year since the Incarnation of the Lord, when a suit was moved between John the lord prior, and the religious community of St Andrews, on the one side, over land that Robert King bequeathed in his last will to the said canons serving God and Saint Andrew, for the sake of his own soul and of his ancestors’ and successors’, and Goda, the daughter of the brother of the said Robert on the other side, an agreement of the following nature was made between the parties in full court;

namely, that the said prior and religious community have given to the said Goda thirty shillings sterling as an exchange; and they have given and also granted to the same Goda and to her heirs a land in the toun of Aberdeen, which Robert Petith donated to the said canons for God and his soul independently, or the rent of the aforementioned land to be received annually, paying yearly from the said land, for the sake of brotherly love, to the aforementioned canons serving God and Saint Andrew, one pound of cumin at the fair of the apostles Peter and Paul. The said Goda has sworn in full court for herself and her heirs that she would faithfully abide by all the aforesaid things, and that she shall warrant the said land to the aforementioned prior and canons against all men and women; and the aforenamed prior and religious community shall warrant to the said Goda and her heirs that they will faithfully abide by all the aforesaid things without scruple or deceit.

The prior and community caused the common seal of the chapter to be affixed to the part of the agreement which remains with Goda and her heirs. To the other part was affixed the common seal of the toun of St Andrews.
As witnesses: the chapter of St Andrews, and the court of the same toun.
